contents.value: **function** — Julia keyword.
**end** — Julia keyword.
**if** — Julia keyword.
**elseif** — Julia keyword.
**else** — Julia keyword.
**println** — Built-in function/command in Julia.
**print** — Built-in function/command in Julia.
**typeof** — Built-in function/command in Julia.
**sizeof** — Built-in function/command in Julia.
**length** — Built-in function/command in Julia.
A **variable** stores a value that can be referenced and modified.
A **loop** repeats a block of code while a condition is true or for each item in a collection.
A **conditional** executes different code branches based on a boolean test.
A **string** is a sequence of characters used for text.
An **array** (or list) is an ordered collection of elements.
A **dictionary** (hash/map) stores key-value pairs for fast lookup.
A **class** defines a blueprint for creating objects with attributes and methods.
A **module** is a file containing reusable code that can be imported.
An **exception** signals an error condition that can be caught and handled.
A **lambda** is a concise anonymous function expression.
A **closure** captures variables from its enclosing scope.
An **iterator** yields values one at a time from a sequence.
A **generator** lazily produces values using yield.
A **decorator** wraps a function to extend its behavior.

```julia
Array{T,N}(undef, dims...)
```
The fundamental multidimensional container type parameterized by element type T and dimensionality N. Arrays in Julia are column-major, mutable, and support broadcasting, comprehensions, and high-performance linear algebra operations through BLAS integration.

```julia
Dict{K,V}(pairs...)
```
A hash-table-based dictionary mapping keys of type K to values of type V. Supports iteration over key-value pairs, efficient lookup, insertion, and deletion. Dict is the standard mutable associative container in Julia's Base library.

```julia
String(data::AbstractVector{UInt8})
```
An immutable UTF-8 encoded string type. Strings in Julia support Unicode natively, are indexed by byte position (not character index), and provide extensive pattern matching, interpolation with $ syntax, and conversion utilities.

```julia
Tuple{T1, T2, ...}
```
An immutable, fixed-size, ordered collection of elements, each potentially having a different type. Tuples are used extensively for multiple return values, function argument packing, and as dictionary keys due to their immutability and efficient memory layout.

```julia
Set{T}(itr)
```
An unordered collection of unique elements of type T, backed by a Dict. Sets support standard mathematical operations including union, intersection, difference, and symmetric difference, as well as membership testing with the in operator.

```julia
struct TypeName fields... end | mutable struct TypeName fields... end
```
Declares a composite type with named fields. Immutable structs (the default) are stack-allocated and more efficiently optimized by the compiler. Mutable structs are heap-allocated and allow field reassignment. Both support constructors, type parameters, and abstract supertype declarations.

```julia
Channel{T}(size::Int=0)
```
A typed concurrent communication primitive used for passing messages between asynchronous tasks. Channels can be buffered (with a specified size) or unbuffered, and support put!/take! operations for producer-consumer patterns in Julia's cooperative multitasking model.

```julia
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```julia
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```julia
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```julia
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.

```julia
undefined
```
Represents an uninitialized or missing value.
